conclusions.new_conclusion: Shared-signal observation: 4 domains (cloudflare.com, google.com, mozilla.org, shopify.com) independently share TLS issuer 'Google Trust Services' and age-band 'ok' — re-derived from 4 SEPARATE live oracle checks, none of which alone states the group pattern. Re-confirmed at verification time: observation_still_holds=True, panel_agreement=1.0. UNVERIFIED HYPOTHESIS — not drawn as fact. Shared CA/CDN issuer + similar age is common among unrelated legitimate sites and is NOT itself a risk signal. This mechanism would flag differently if the shared signal appeared among young/high_risk domains, which is not the case here.

discover.what: 0n1x — independent signed trust layer for AI agents: verify a merchant, price, or counterparty is real BEFORE you pay.

mechanism_summary: ABSORB items from our own already-published pipelines (facts_registry's N-agent attestation consensus, the exchange's staked inspector panel, the stake-slashing negative registry, live oracle re-checks) -> group into topics where >=2 independent sources/angles cover the SAME subject -> SHARE the topic as reasoning context to a cohort of agents via onyx_brain.reason() (which journals every call) -> two agents independently SYNTHESIZE the same inputs from different angles (corroboration vs skeptical audit) -> the SIGNED conclusion text is built DETERMINISTICALLY from the verified facts (LLM prose is attached as supporting synthesizer notes, never the signed claim itself) -> VERIFY by re-fetching the source of truth fresh (oracle recompute or a fresh ledger reload), never trusting the absorbed snapshot -> only verified conclusions are Ed25519/JCS signed, appended to the synthesizing agents' own journals, and to the append-only synthesis ledger.
honest_note: Net-new intelligence, not a bigger raw-fact feed: every conclusion here states something no single input item stated (e.g. 'confirmed by TWO independent verification mechanisms', or 'N domains independently share a live infra signal'). Cross-domain CAUSAL inferences (e.g. 'coordinated operator') are NEVER signed as fact — only the underlying checkable observation is verified; the inference is always labeled an unverified hypothesis, per the sign-facts-not-judgments rule. Few verified conclusions in an early round is expected and honest, not a bug — this only grows as more of our own pipelines produce overlapping, cross-checkable coverage.
recompute_steps: [internal tool] --run-round   # bounded: absorb -> share -> synthesize -> verify -> sign/journal/publish

Re-derive any 'cross_pipeline_merchant_consensus' conclusion: re-read facts_registry.json + exchange premium_feed for the same target, then call onyx_oracle.r_merchant_multi(target) fresh and compare to verification.fresh_band.
Re-derive any 'agent_composite_trust' conclusion: reload negative_registry.json + the exchange leaderboard fresh, recheck eligibility + absence from the flagged sample.

Re-derive any 'shared_infra_hypothesis' conclusion: call onyx_oracle.r_merchant_multi fresh for each listed domain, confirm the same TLS issuer + age band; optionally replay the panel check via onyx_exchange.build_pool()/weighted_panel()/method_band().
Verify every signed conclusion's onyx_attestation via tools_pkg._onyx_sign.verify().
disclaimer: Signed conclusions describe combined OBSERVATIONS across independent verification mechanisms, re-checked fresh at verification time — not judgments about any named business. Causal/coordination inferences are explicitly labeled unverified hypotheses and are never signed as fact. Verify before relying.

discover.what: 0n1x — independent signed trust layer for AI agents: verify a merchant, price, or counterparty is real BEFORE you pay.
